wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the InfraWatch project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a real-time monitoring platform for Solana infrastructure health, its target audience (validator operators, app builders, retail stakers), and key benefits. Document the core value proposition of providing comprehensive network monitoring, RPC provider health tracking, and validator performance analytics. Include the project's mission to enhance transparency and reliability in the Solana ecosystem through real-time data visualization and alerting capabilities. Provide context about the Solana blockchain ecosystem and why infrastructure monitoring is crucial for participants.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed component architecture documentation for InfraWatch frontend. Document the component hierarchy starting with AppShell as the main layout container, followed by reusable components in common/, dashboard-specific components, RPC monitoring components, and validator components. Explain component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and component lifecycle management. Detail the layout system with Header and Sidebar components, and how they integrate with page-level components. Include component styling approaches, responsive design patterns, and accessibility implementations. Document component reusability strategies and prop drilling prevention through the store architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Network API endpoints. Document the GET /api/network/current endpoint for retrieving real-time network status including TPS, slot height, latency, epoch progress, validator counts, and congestion metrics. Detail the GET /api/network/history endpoint with range parameters (1h, 24h, 7d) for historical data retrieval. Include request/response schemas, parameter validation, caching strategy with Redis fallback, error handling patterns, and example responses. Explain the cache-first architecture, data transformation logic, and performance considerations for real-time network monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Current Network Status Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the GET /api/network/current endpoint. Document the cache-first architecture using Redis with fallback to PostgreSQL database. Detail the response schema including status, tps, slotHeight, slotLatencyMs, epoch, epochProgress, delinquentCount, activeValidators, confirmationTimeMs, congestionScore, and timestamp fields. Explain the data transformation logic from cache objects to API responses, error handling for Redis unavailability and database startup phases, and the health status determination. Include example request/response pairs, caching behavior, and performance considerations for real-time network monitoring dashboards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Setup & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for InfraWatch server setup and configuration. Document the main server entry point (server.js), Express application initialization, HTTP server creation, and Socket.io WebSocket server setup. Explain middleware configuration including Helmet for security headers, compression for performance, CORS settings, and JSON parsing. Detail the configuration management system, environment variable handling, and security considerations. Include server startup procedures, graceful shutdown handling, and health check endpoint implementation. Document the global Socket.io instance setup and its availability to other modules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Solana RPC Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Solana RPC Service implementation. Document the blockchain data collection functionality including network health monitoring, TPS calculation, slot progression tracking, epoch information retrieval, delinquent validator detection, and confirmation time estimation. Explain the Connection class initialization, RPC method usage patterns, error handling strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Detail the congestion score calculation algorithm combining TPS, priority fees, and slot latency metrics. Include examples of network snapshot collection, concurrent data fetching using Promise.all, and slot tracking mechanisms. Document the mathematical formulas used for congestion scoring and provide code examples for each service method.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Abstraction Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the PostgreSQL database abstraction layer. Detail the node-postgres Pool implementation, connection management, and lazy initialization patterns. Document the query execution system, parameter binding, and error handling strategies. Explain the database migration system including schema evolution, migration scripts, and version management. Cover connection pooling configuration, timeout settings, and resource management. Include examples of CRUD operations, transaction handling, and bulk data processing. Document the query builder patterns, prepared statements, and performance optimization techniques. Address database connectivity issues, connection recovery, and monitoring approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Critical Poller (30s Intervals)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Critical Poller implementation. Document the 30-second polling mechanism using node-cron scheduler, explaining the sequential data collection workflow: network snapshot collection, Helius priority fee enhancement, RPC provider probing, database persistence, Redis caching, and WebSocket broadcasting. Detail the job execution pattern with concurrency control (isRunning flag), graceful error handling for database and Redis failures, and the complete data processing pipeline. Include examples of the collected metrics (TPS, slot latency, congestion score, RPC health), cache key management, and real-time broadcasting to connected clients. Explain the critical nature of this job as the system heartbeat and its impact on overall system responsiveness.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Routes
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the network monitoring endpoints. Document all network-related endpoints including TPS tracking, slot latency monitoring, epoch progress data, and network congestion metrics. Detail the HTTP methods, URL patterns (/api/network/*), request/response schemas, and data structures returned. Explain the network snapshot data format, historical data retrieval patterns, and real-time updates via WebSocket integration. Include examples of querying network statistics, filtering by time ranges, and handling pagination for historical data. Document error responses, rate limiting, and caching strategies for network data endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Health Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Network Health Dashboard feature. Detail the TPS monitoring implementation with real-time transaction processing metrics and historical trend analysis. Explain slot latency tracking showing block confirmation delays and network responsiveness. Document confirmation time card displaying average transaction settlement duration. Cover congestion score calculation based on network traffic and fee market dynamics. Include epoch progress visualization with epoch timeline and upcoming epoch events. Explain the TPS history chart with configurable time ranges and performance benchmarking. Document the network status banner with overall health indicators and alert conditions. Provide implementation details for data fetching, state management, and real-time updates via WebSocket.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for InfraWatch. Include detailed environment setup instructions, required dependencies (Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis), and step-by-step installation process. Document environment variable configuration, database initialization, and Redis setup. Provide clear instructions for both backend and frontend development environments. Include initial project setup verification steps, local development server startup procedures, and basic usage examples. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting tips for new developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive state management documentation for InfraWatch using Zustand stores. Document the three main stores: networkStore for network-wide metrics, rpcStore for RPC provider data, and validatorStore for validator information. Explain store structure, state shape, actions, and selectors. Detail how stores integrate with React components through hooks, state subscription patterns, and performance optimizations. Document state persistence strategies, store initialization, and cleanup procedures. Include examples of store usage in components, state update patterns, and debugging techniques for state management issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: RPC API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the RPC API endpoints. Document endpoints for RPC provider health monitoring, performance metrics collection, and provider recommendations. Include request/response schemas for retrieving RPC provider status, uptime statistics, latency measurements, and health check results. Detail the RPC prober service integration, provider scoring algorithms, and failover recommendation logic. Provide examples of provider selection criteria, performance thresholds, and integration patterns for application developers building on Solana infrastructure.

wiki_catalogs.name: Historical Network Data Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the GET /api/network/history endpoint. Document the range parameter validation (1h, 24h, 7d) and the cache-first approach with Redis caching using dynamic cache keys. Detail the response format as arrays of network snapshot objects with timestamped data points. Explain the caching strategy with TTL configuration, cache key generation using cacheKeys.NETWORK_HISTORY(range), and the fallback mechanism to database queries. Include parameter validation errors, empty response handling when database is unavailable, and the data transformation process for chart rendering. Provide examples of response data structures and caching behavior patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the InfraWatch routing system. Document the Express router architecture, route organization, and endpoint structure. Explain the modular routing pattern with separate route files for different domains (network, RPC, validators, epoch, alerts). Detail each API endpoint specification including HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Document route middleware usage, parameter validation, and error handling at the route level. Include examples of route composition, nested routing patterns, and how routes integrate with the service layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Helius Integration Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Helius integration service that enhances Solana RPC data collection. Document the API integration patterns, data enrichment capabilities, and priority fee analysis functionality. Explain the Helius API authentication, request formatting, response parsing, and error handling mechanisms. Detail the priority fee percentile calculations (low, medium, high, veryHigh, 90th percentile) and their role in network congestion analysis. Include examples of API configuration, request throttling, retry logic, and data transformation processes. Document the integration workflow between Helius service and Solana RPC service for comprehensive network monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Redis Caching Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Redis caching layer implementation. Document the Redis connection management, client configuration, and connection pooling strategies. Explain the caching strategy including TTL configuration, cache key management, and data serialization patterns. Detail the cache key naming conventions, hierarchical key organization, and cache invalidation strategies. Document the data structures used for different types of cached data including network snapshots, RPC health checks, and validator information. Include examples of cache operations, bulk cache management, and cache warming procedures. Cover cache performance optimization, memory management, and monitoring techniques. Address cache consistency patterns, stale data handling, and cache failure scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routine Poller (Lower Frequency)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Routine Poller implementation. Document the lower-frequency polling mechanism designed for less critical but important data collection tasks. Explain the routine poller's role in collecting validator data from Validators.app API, processing validator information, and updating long-term data stores. Detail the scheduling strategy, data processing workflows, and integration with the validator monitoring system. Include examples of validator data collection patterns, API rate limiting considerations, and the relationship between routine polling and the critical 30-second heartbeat. Document the job's contribution to maintaining comprehensive validator health tracking and historical data accumulation.

wiki_catalogs.name: RPC Routes
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for RPC provider monitoring endpoints. Document all RPC-related endpoints covering provider health checks, uptime statistics, latency measurements, and performance metrics. Detail the HTTP methods, URL patterns (/api/rpc/*), request parameters, and response schemas for RPC provider data. Explain the RPC health check data structure, provider ranking algorithms, failover recommendations, and geographic distribution mapping. Include examples of querying provider performance, filtering by region or uptime thresholds, and retrieving historical health data. Document authentication requirements, rate limiting policies, and WebSocket integration for real-time RPC status updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: RPC Provider Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for RPC Provider Monitoring functionality. Explain the RPC provider table implementation with provider health metrics, uptime statistics, and performance benchmarks. Document the RPC recommendation banner with provider selection guidance and failover suggestions. Detail the real-time health checking system for RPC endpoints including latency measurements and availability monitoring. Cover provider performance scoring based on response times, error rates, and consistency metrics. Explain failover recommendation algorithms that suggest alternative RPC providers during outages. Document the data collection pipeline from backend polling to frontend display. Include provider selection criteria for different use cases such as read operations, write operations, and high-throughput scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for InfraWatch's system design. Describe the full-stack architecture with Node.js/Express backend and React/Vite frontend. Document the microservices pattern, component relationships, and data flow architecture. Explain the real-time communication via WebSocket, background job processing, and external API integrations. Include system boundaries, integration patterns, and technical decisions. Document the separation of concerns between frontend components, backend services, and database layers. Address scalability considerations, deployment topology, and infrastructure requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed routing and navigation documentation for InfraWatch. Document the React Router configuration with AppShell as the main layout wrapper and individual page components for each feature area. Explain the nested routing structure, route parameters, and navigation patterns. Detail how AppShell provides consistent layout across pages, including header navigation and sidebar integration. Document programmatic navigation, route guards, and URL parameter handling. Include navigation UX patterns, breadcrumb implementation, and responsive navigation behaviors for different screen sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validators API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Validators API endpoints. Document endpoints for validator information retrieval, performance scoring, delinquency tracking, and commission data. Include request/response schemas for validator profiles, performance metrics, ranking systems, and governance participation data. Detail the integration with Validators.app API, validator scoring algorithms, delinquency monitoring logic, and commission change tracking. Provide examples of validator selection criteria, performance benchmarks, and delegation decision support patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the InfraWatch service layer architecture. Document the service layer pattern implementation, external API integration modules, and business logic encapsulation. Explain each service module including Solana RPC service for blockchain data collection, Helius integration for enhanced RPC metrics, Validators.app integration for validator information, and RPC prober service for provider health checks. Detail service initialization, configuration management, error handling strategies, and retry mechanisms. Document inter-service communication patterns, data transformation processes, and caching integration. Include examples of service usage, dependency injection, and testing strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validators.app Integration Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Validators.app integration service that provides validator information and scoring. Document the API integration patterns, validator data retrieval, performance metrics collection, and scoring algorithms. Explain the Validators.app API authentication, data schema mapping, and real-time updates mechanism. Detail the validator information structure including performance scores, delinquency tracking, commission rates, voting history, and geographic distribution data. Include examples of validator data processing, scoring calculations, and integration with the main validator monitoring system. Document error handling, rate limiting, and data caching strategies specific to the Validators.app API.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validators Routes
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for validator monitoring endpoints. Document all validator-related endpoints including validator performance scoring, delinquency tracking, commission changes, voting history, and stake distribution. Detail the HTTP methods, URL patterns (/api/validators/*), request parameters, and response schemas for validator data. Explain the validator score calculation methodology, performance metrics, delinquency thresholds, and governance participation tracking. Include examples of querying validator rankings, filtering by performance criteria, retrieving detailed validator profiles, and monitoring commission changes over time. Document data freshness guarantees, caching strategies, and WebSocket updates for real-time validator status changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validator Health Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Validator Health Tracking system. Detail the validator table implementation with performance metrics, voting participation rates, and commission information. Explain the validator detail panel showing comprehensive health indicators including uptime, stake distribution, and delegation activity. Document the validator score badge system with performance ratings and risk assessments. Cover validator performance scoring algorithms based on voting efficiency, uptime reliability, and community feedback. Explain delinquency monitoring with automatic detection of missed slots and penalties. Detail commission change tracking for delegation decision-making. Document validator ranking systems and filtering capabilities. Include integration with external validator data sources and real-time performance updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend architecture documentation for InfraWatch. Document the Node.js/Express server setup, configuration management, and modular service layer. Explain the MVC-like pattern implementation, routing structure, and middleware architecture. Detail the service layer pattern for external API integrations, database abstraction layer, and WebSocket communication setup. Document the background job system for data collection, caching strategy with Redis, and error handling mechanisms. Include component interactions, data flows, and integration patterns with external services like Solana RPC, Helius, and Validators.app.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API integration documentation for InfraWatch frontend. Document the service layer architecture with centralized API clients for network, RPC, and validator data. Explain the axios-based API configuration, request/response handling, and error management. Detail the WebSocket integration through useWebSocket hook for real-time data updates. Document API client patterns, request interceptors, response transformers, and error handling strategies. Include examples of API usage in components, loading states, and data caching strategies. Explain authentication patterns, API versioning, and rate limiting considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Epoch API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Epoch API endpoints. Document endpoints for epoch timing information, epoch progress tracking, and epoch-related network data. Include request/response schemas for epoch start/end times, current epoch number, epoch progress percentage, and upcoming epoch events. Detail the integration with Solana RPC for accurate epoch timing, epoch transition detection, and epoch-based analytics. Provide examples of epoch-aware application development, timing-sensitive operations, and epoch-based reward calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Persistence Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the InfraWatch data persistence layer. Document the database abstraction layer using PostgreSQL, Redis caching implementation, and data access patterns. Explain the database connection management, query execution, and transaction handling. Detail the Redis caching strategy including TTL configuration, cache key management, and data serialization. Document the database migration system, schema evolution, and data lifecycle management. Include cache invalidation strategies, data consistency patterns, and performance optimization techniques. Provide examples of CRUD operations, bulk data processing, and cache warming procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: RPC Prober Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the RPC Prober Service that performs health checks and performance monitoring of RPC providers. Document the probing algorithms, latency measurement techniques, uptime tracking, and failover detection mechanisms. Explain the concurrent probing strategies, timeout configurations, and response validation processes. Detail the RPC provider health scoring system, performance benchmarking, and geographic location correlation. Include examples of probe scheduling, result aggregation, alert triggering, and integration with the main RPC monitoring dashboard. Document the probe configuration options, failure detection thresholds, and automated remediation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Epoch Routes
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for epoch and staking cycle endpoints. Document all epoch-related endpoints covering epoch progress tracking, reward calculations, stake lockup periods, and validator rotation schedules. Detail the HTTP methods, URL patterns (/api/epoch/*), request parameters, and response schemas for epoch data. Explain the epoch timeline visualization, upcoming epoch events, reward distribution schedules, and staking cycle management. Include examples of querying current epoch information, historical epoch data, reward projections, and validator schedule changes. Document epoch boundary detection, data synchronization with Solana network, and WebSocket notifications for epoch transitions.

wiki_catalogs.name: MEV Exposure Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for MEV (Maximal Extractable Value) Exposure Monitoring feature. Explain Jito validator detection system with automated identification of MEV-protected validator nodes. Document tip floor tracking showing minimum transaction fees and market manipulation indicators. Detail MEV-protected RPC identification for secure transaction submission. Cover MEV exposure scoring that quantifies potential extraction risks for different RPC providers. Explain the relationship between MEV activity and network congestion patterns. Document real-time MEV activity monitoring with alert thresholds and risk warnings. Include educational content explaining MEV concepts, risks, and mitigation strategies for users and developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for InfraWatch. Document the React/Vite application structure, component hierarchy, and state management with Zustand stores. Explain the page-based routing system, reusable component library, and API integration layer. Detail the WebSocket client implementation, real-time data handling, and UI state synchronization. Document the component composition patterns, prop drilling prevention strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Include the styling approach, responsive design principles, and accessibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Alerts API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Alerts API endpoints. Document endpoints for alert configuration, alert subscription management, and alert notification delivery. Include request/response schemas for creating alerts, managing user preferences, configuring notification channels, and receiving real-time alert notifications via WebSocket. Detail the alert trigger conditions, notification delivery mechanisms, integration with external systems (Bags chat), and alert lifecycle management. Provide examples of custom alert configurations, webhook integration patterns, and alert escalation procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Background Jobs & Data Collection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the InfraWatch background job system and data collection pipeline. Document the critical poller (30-second intervals) and routine poller implementations, job scheduling mechanisms, and data collection workflows. Explain the polling strategies for network metrics, RPC health checks, validator data updates, and external API integrations. Detail job execution patterns, error handling, retry logic, and performance monitoring. Document the coordination between pollers, data processing pipelines, and cache updates. Include job configuration, resource management, and scaling considerations. Provide examples of adding new pollers, modifying collection intervals, and debugging data collection issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Alerts Routes
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for alert management endpoints. Document all alert-related endpoints including alert creation, subscription management, notification delivery, and user preferences. Detail the HTTP methods, URL patterns (/api/alerts/*), request/response schemas, and alert configuration options. Explain the alert trigger conditions, notification channels (Bags chat integration), user preference management, and alert history tracking. Include examples of setting up custom alerts, managing alert subscriptions, configuring notification preferences, and retrieving alert history. Document authentication requirements, rate limiting for alert notifications, and integration patterns with external messaging systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Center Risk Assessment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Data Center Risk Assessment functionality. Detail the geographic distribution mapping showing RPC provider locations and data center concentrations. Explain concentration risk calculation based on geographic clustering and single points of failure analysis. Document risk alert systems that notify users of high-risk geographic regions. Cover the integration with mapping libraries for interactive geographic visualization. Explain data center reliability metrics and regional infrastructure quality assessments. Detail the correlation between geographic diversity and network resilience. Include risk scoring algorithms that quantify geographic concentration risks and provide mitigation recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for InfraWatch's REST endpoints. Document all HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint group. Include detailed parameter specifications, response formats, error codes, and example requests/responses. Cover the network API for health metrics, RPC API for provider monitoring, validators API for performance data, epoch API for timing information, and alerts API for notification management. Document rate limiting, pagination, filtering options, and data validation rules. Provide client implementation examples and integration guidelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the InfraWatch WebSocket communication system. Document the Socket.io implementation, real-time data broadcasting, and client connection management. Explain WebSocket server setup, connection handling, event emission patterns, and client-server communication protocols. Detail the real-time data streaming architecture, message formatting, and event-driven updates. Document connection lifecycle management, authentication for WebSocket connections, and error handling strategies. Include examples of broadcasting network updates, handling client subscriptions, and managing connection scaling. Provide guidance on WebSocket debugging, performance optimization, and integration with the frontend real-time components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Alert System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Alert System functionality. Explain alert configuration options including threshold settings, notification preferences, and alert types. Document alert management interface for creating, editing, and deleting user-defined alerts. Detail integration with external notification channels including chat bots and messaging systems. Cover alert triggering mechanisms based on network conditions, RPC provider failures, and validator health changes. Explain alert history tracking and notification logs. Document user preference management for customizing alert frequencies and delivery methods. Include API endpoints for alert CRUD operations and webhook integration for external systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for InfraWatch's database schema and caching strategy. Detail entity relationships, field definitions, data types, and constraints for network snapshots, RPC health checks, and validator information. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, and database indexes. Explain data validation rules, business logic constraints, and data lifecycle management. Include PostgreSQL schema diagrams, sample data examples, and data access patterns. Document Redis cache implementation, cache key management, TTL configuration, and caching strategies for real-time data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Middleware & Error Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the InfraWatch middleware and error handling system. Document the custom error handling middleware, 404 not found handling, and global error management patterns. Explain middleware chain execution order, request/response processing, and error propagation strategies. Detail error classification, logging mechanisms, and response formatting for different error types. Document security middleware including CORS configuration, Helmet security headers, and input validation. Include examples of custom middleware creation, error handling best practices, and debugging techniques. Provide guidance on extending the middleware stack and implementing additional security or monitoring layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Features & Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive feature documentation for InfraWatch's core functionality. Document the network health dashboard with TPS monitoring, slot latency tracking, and epoch progress visualization. Explain RPC provider monitoring with health checks, performance metrics, and failover recommendations. Detail validator health tracking with performance scoring, delinquency monitoring, and commission change alerts. Cover MEV exposure monitoring for Jito validators and tip floor tracking. Document data center risk assessment with geographic distribution mapping and concentration risk calculation. Include alert system configuration and notification management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Data Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for InfraWatch's real-time data flow architecture. Document the data collection pipeline from critical pollers to external API integrations, data processing, and caching strategies. Explain the WebSocket broadcasting system for live updates, client connection management, and event handling. Detail the background job scheduling, data synchronization patterns, and real-time state management. Include data transformation workflows, cache invalidation strategies, and performance optimization techniques for real-time data delivery.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development documentation for InfraWatch contributors. Document the development workflow, coding standards, and best practices for both backend and frontend development. Include component development guidelines, service layer implementation patterns, and state management conventions. Detail testing strategies, debugging techniques, and performance profiling approaches. Document the build process, deployment procedures, and continuous integration practices. Include contribution guidelines, code review processes, and development environment setup for team collaboration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and maintenance documentation for InfraWatch. Document production deployment strategies, containerization options, and infrastructure requirements. Include database maintenance procedures, cache management, and log rotation strategies. Detail monitoring setup, health check endpoints, and performance metrics collection. Document backup procedures, disaster recovery planning, and system maintenance schedules. Include troubleshooting guides for common operational issues, performance optimization techniques, and scaling considerations for production environments.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# InfraWatch: Real-Time Solana Infrastructure Monitoring Dashboard

## Project Introduction

InfraWatch is a comprehensive real-time monitoring platform designed to track and visualize the health of Solana's infrastructure layer. Built specifically for the Bags ecosystem, it provides critical insights into RPC node reliability, validator performance, network congestion, and MEV exposure that directly impacts every application built on top of Solana.

### Purpose Statement
The platform addresses a fundamental gap in the Solana ecosystem: while 170+ hackathon teams have built applications on top of Solana's infrastructure, none have monitored the underlying infrastructure itself. When RPC endpoints fail, trading bots fail silently. When validators become delinquent, staking rewards drop. When networks congest, transactions fail.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Infrastructure Visibility**: Provide live monitoring of Solana's core infrastructure components
- **Bags Ecosystem Integration**: Seamlessly integrate with the Bags ecosystem through native chat alerts and fee-sharing tokens
- **Multi-dimensional Monitoring**: Track RPC health, validator performance, network congestion, and MEV exposure
- **Proactive Alerting**: Notify users of critical infrastructure events before they impact applications

### Target Audience
- **Validator Operators**: Need real-time monitoring of their node performance and network conditions
- **Bags App Builders**: Require reliable infrastructure data to ensure their applications remain functional
- **Retail Stakers**: Want visibility into the health of validators they delegate to
- **Infrastructure Researchers**: Need comprehensive data about Solana's operational health

## Technical Architecture

InfraWatch follows a modern microservice architecture with clear separation between frontend, backend, and data layers:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[React Frontend] --> B[Express.js API]
    B --> C[Socket.io Server]
    C --> D[Data Collection Services]
    D --> E[PostgreSQL Database]
    D --> F[Redis Cache]
    D --> G[External APIs]
    
    H[Helius API] --> D
    I[Validators.app API] --> D
    J[Solana RPC] --> D
    K[Bags API] --> D
    
    L[Custom RPC Prober] --> D
    M[Background Jobs] --> D
```

### Component Breakdown

**Backend Layer (Node.js/Express)**
- REST API with comprehensive route structure
- WebSocket server for real-time updates
- Background job scheduling for data collection
- Modular service architecture for different data sources

**Frontend Layer (React/Vite)**
- Component-based architecture with reusable UI elements
- State management using Zustand
- Real-time updates via WebSocket connections
- Responsive design optimized for desktop and mobile

**Data Layer**
- PostgreSQL for historical time-series data
- Redis for real-time caching and session management
- External API integrations for comprehensive data coverage

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key architectural patterns:

- **Service Layer Pattern**: Each data source is encapsulated in dedicated service modules
- **Observer Pattern**: WebSocket connections notify clients of infrastructure changes
- **Factory Pattern**: Configuration management handles different environments and data sources
- **Repository Pattern**: Database operations are abstracted behind clean interfaces

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Server** (`backend/server.js`)
The primary entry point initializes the entire application stack, including Express server setup, WebSocket configuration, middleware registration, and background job scheduling.

**Frontend Application** (`frontend/src/App.jsx`)
Defines the React Router configuration and component hierarchy for the dashboard interface.

### Core Modules

**Configuration Management** (`backend/src/config/index.js`)
Centralized configuration handling with environment variable support and sensible defaults for all external services.

**Data Collection Services** (`backend/src/services/`)
- `solanaRpc.js`: Direct Solana RPC interactions for network metrics
- `helius.js`: Enhanced data collection via Helius API for priority fees and TPS
- `validatorsApp.js`: Integration with Validators.app for validator data
- `rpcProber.js`: Custom RPC endpoint health checking

**Background Jobs** (`backend/src/jobs/`)
- `criticalPoller.js`: High-frequency data collection (every 30 seconds)
- `routinePoller.js`: Lower-frequency comprehensive data processing

**WebSocket Communication** (`backend/src/websocket/`)
Real-time bidirectional communication between server and clients for live dashboard updates.

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies** (`backend/package.json`)
- `@solana/web3.js`: Official Solana JavaScript SDK
- `socket.io`: Real-time bidirectional communication
- `node-cron`: Background job scheduling
- `ioredis`: Redis client for caching
- `express`: Web application framework
- `helmet`: Security headers
- `compression`: Response compression

**Frontend Dependencies** (`frontend/package.json`)
- `react` & `react-dom`: User interface framework
- `react-router-dom`: Client-side routing
- `socket.io-client`: WebSocket client for real-time updates
- `zustand`: Lightweight state management
- `recharts`: Data visualization components
- `react-leaflet`: Geographic data mapping

### Integration Points

**API Endpoints** (`backend/src/routes/`)
- `/api/network`: Network health and metrics
- `/api/rpc`: RPC endpoint health and performance
- `/api/validators`: Validator information and scoring
- `/api/epoch`: Epoch information and progress
- `/api/alerts`: Alert management and configuration

**Data Flow Architecture**

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Cron as Critical Poller
    participant Solana as Solana RPC
    participant Helius as Helius API
    participant DB as PostgreSQL
    participant Redis as Redis Cache
    participant WS as WebSocket
    
    Cron->>+Solana: Collect Network Snapshot
    Solana-->>-Cron: Network Metrics
    Cron->>+Helius: Get Priority Fees
    Helius-->>-Cron: Fee Data
    Cron->>+DB: Insert Historical Data
    DB-->>-Cron: Acknowledgment
    Cron->>+Redis: Cache Live Data
    Redis-->>-Cron: Acknowledgment
    Cron->>+WS: Broadcast Updates
    WS-->>-Cron: Client Notifications
```

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Server.js] --> B[Routes]
    B --> C[Services]
    C --> D[Models]
    C --> E[WebSocket]
    C --> F[Jobs]
    
    G[Frontend] --> H[API Layer]
    H --> I[Socket.io]
    I --> J[Live Updates]
    
    K[Configuration] --> A
    K --> C
    K --> G
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Network Health Dashboard**
- Real-time TPS monitoring with historical trends
- Slot latency tracking and congestion detection
- Epoch progress visualization with ETA calculations
- Delinquent validator count and network status indicators

**RPC Provider Monitoring**
- Comprehensive health checks for major Solana RPC providers
- Latency measurements and uptime statistics
- Automated failover recommendations based on current conditions
- Performance comparisons across different providers

**Validator Health Tracking**
- Real-time validator performance metrics
- Commission change notifications
- Software version and client diversity tracking
- Geographic distribution and concentration risk assessment

**MEV Exposure Monitoring**
- Jito-enabled validator identification
- MEV tip floor tracking and trends
- MEV-protected RPC provider detection
- Plain-English explanations of MEV implications

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Data Collection** (`backend/src/jobs/criticalPoller.js`)
The critical poller runs every 30 seconds, collecting comprehensive network snapshots and broadcasting updates to all connected clients. This ensures the dashboard remains current without overwhelming external APIs.

**Intelligent Caching Strategy** (`backend/src/models/redis.js`)
Redis serves as a high-performance cache layer, storing frequently accessed data and reducing API call frequency. The cache uses different TTL values for critical and routine data.

**Comprehensive Error Handling** (`backend/src/middleware/errorHandler.js`)
Robust error handling ensures the system remains operational even when individual data sources fail. Graceful degradation maintains partial functionality during outages.

**Responsive Frontend Architecture** (`frontend/src/components/`)
The React-based frontend provides a responsive, real-time dashboard experience with reusable components for consistent user interaction across different screen sizes.

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initializing
    Initializing --> DataCollection: Server Start
    DataCollection --> Processing: New Metrics
    Processing --> Caching: Processed Data
    Processing --> Broadcasting: Live Updates
    Caching --> Storage: Historical Data
    Broadcasting --> Clients: Real-time Updates
    Storage --> [*]: Data Persistence
    Clients --> DataCollection: Next Cycle
```

The system operates on a continuous loop of data collection, processing, caching, and broadcasting, ensuring users always have access to the most current infrastructure information.

---

- [solanaRpc.js](backend/src/services/solanaRpc.js)
- [criticalPoller.js](backend/src/jobs/criticalPoller.js)
- [App.jsx](frontend/src/App.jsx)
- [networkStore.js](frontend/src/stores/networkStore.js)
- [infrawatch_build_plan.md](infrawatch_build_plan.md)

- [helius.js](backend/src/services/helius.js)

</blog>
